program.targetPhase1Design: Single ascending dose followed by a short multiple ascending dose in healthy volunteers, with PK-rich sampling and optional CSF biomarker exploration if exposure supports it.
program.targetIndStrategy: Tell a coherent IND story around oral exposure, CNS penetration, early efficacy plausibility, formulation continuity, and a defensible nonclinical safety package.

nodes.title: Mouse PK / brain exposure bridge
nodes.objective: Confirm that oral dosing reaches free brain exposure compatible with the intended Phase 1 path.
nodes.procedureSummary: Single-dose mouse PK with plasma and brain sampling across 8 hours using the intended suspension formulation.
nodes.successCriteria: Free brain exposure clears the projected efficacy threshold with interpretable variability.
nodes.decisionSupported: Supports brain penetration claim and early dose framing.
nodes.results: Observed free brain exposure exceeded the projected efficacy threshold for approximately 3 hours after oral dosing. Variability was acceptable, but exposure dropped quickly at the lowest dose level.
nodes.operationalNotes: Bioanalysis run completed on time; repeat matrix standardization is not required.
nodes.phase1Relevance: Supports the oral SAD design and whether CSF biomarker sampling is worth carrying into the protocol.
nodes.indRelevance: Provides the core exposure bridge for the clinical pharmacology narrative.

nodes.title: Suspension formulation and analytical bridge
nodes.objective: Lock the nonclinical formulation and assay package that will carry through tox and IND-enabling work.
nodes.procedureSummary: Finalize the suspension composition, release tests, and analytical transfer notes for tox and exposure studies.
nodes.successCriteria: Formulation is reproducible, analytically trackable, and stable over the planned study windows.
nodes.decisionSupported: Supports formulation continuity across the nonclinical package.
nodes.results: Current suspension is stable over 48 hours with acceptable assayability, but viscosity increases above the top dose target.
nodes.operationalNotes: Analytical standard reorder is pending but not yet blocking.
nodes.phase1Relevance: Helps keep the tox and early clinical formulation story aligned.
nodes.indRelevance: Reduces the risk that exposure or safety data need reinterpretation because of formulation drift.

nodes.title: In vivo efficacy bridge
nodes.objective: Confirm that the clinic-bound candidate still shows a credible efficacy signal at achievable exposure.
nodes.procedureSummary: Run the lead in the mouse neuroinflammation model with matched PK sampling at the efficacy readout time point.
nodes.successCriteria: Directionally positive efficacy tied to measured exposure without forcing implausible dose escalation.
nodes.decisionSupported: Supports translational plausibility for the clinic-bound mechanism story.
nodes.results: Interim cohort shows biomarker movement in the expected direction, but exposure-response separation is still shallow.
nodes.operationalNotes: Model slot is reserved; cohort randomization has little slack if formulation changes again.
nodes.phase1Relevance: Helps justify the biomarker readout strategy and exposure assumptions in Phase 1.
nodes.indRelevance: Supports the efficacy plausibility section of the IND narrative.

nodes.title: 14-day dose-range finding tox
nodes.objective: Bound the near-term safety envelope before committing to the IND-enabling path.
nodes.procedureSummary: Short repeat-dose tox study with safety observations, clinical chemistry, and exposure coverage at the intended top dose range.
nodes.successCriteria: Findings remain interpretable and leave a credible margin-setting path into IND-enabling studies.
nodes.decisionSupported: Informs dose selection and de-risks the IND safety narrative.
nodes.operationalNotes: CRO slot is available now but will slip by four weeks if missed.
nodes.phase1Relevance: Directly shapes whether the intended escalation strategy remains credible.
nodes.indRelevance: Core support for the safety narrative in the IND package.

nodes.title: Core safety pharmacology gap check
nodes.objective: Resolve whether the current graph still supports the planned CNS-focused Phase 1 package without a late safety surprise.
nodes.procedureSummary: Scope the minimum follow-up package for CNS, respiratory, and cardiovascular risk based on current findings and open questions.
nodes.successCriteria: Required safety pharmacology work is clearly defined and sequenced without contradicting the current IND story.
nodes.decisionSupported: Clarifies what additional package is needed before locking the IND sequence.
nodes.operationalNotes: Awaiting tox protocol finalization before the final vendor quote can be issued.
nodes.phase1Relevance: Necessary if the planned escalation and monitoring assumptions are to remain credible.
nodes.indRelevance: Prevents a weak or internally inconsistent safety package heading into the IND story.
